hero.question: Will Bitcoin dip to $45,000 by December 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: 46% odds mean the market has genuinely split on whether Bitcoin craters 30%+ in two years, which only happens if you believe either a regulatory nuke lands or macro turns ugly. The crowd's 10-point retreat in 24h screams indecision, but a sharp trader should recognize that Bitcoin's long-term floor keeps rising with institutional adoption, making a $45K dip a sucker's bet unless something structurally breaks.

daily_take.headline: Bitcoin's $45K Floor Looks Safer Than The Market Thinks Right Now
daily_take.deck: Bitcoin dip odds crashed 10 points overnight to 46%, signaling either capitulation or panic selling into strength. The math doesn't match reality, 24-month timeframe gives macro tailwinds room to work, and smart money should be laying this down hard. Market's pricing in recession-grade pain, but positioning looks fragile enough to snap the other way first.
